.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1502:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/post-dispatch-validate.md:91:- [finding-dedup-by-symbol.md](../03--agreement-merge/finding-dedup-by-symbol.md) — only validated findings reach the dedup/agreement layer
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1503:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/post-dispatch-validate.md:92:- [reduce-state-merge.md](../05--context-report-synthesis/reduce-state-merge.md) — broader `reduceContextState` flow within which validation runs
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1504:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/context-report-assembly.md:9:  - "context report JSON"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1505:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/context-report-assembly.md:44:`step_emit_report_json` writes `context-report.json` with the same merged findings as structured JSON, plus per-finding attribution `{producedBy[], agreement, relevance, freshness}` and the final convergence signals. This file is the primary input for automated downstream consumption by `/speckit:plan` and `/speckit:implement`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1506:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/context-report-assembly.md:48:`step_convergence_report` appends a convergence summary to the markdown report: stop reason, total iterations, executor pool, final signal values, relevance gate, agreement min, and convergence threshold. A `synthesis_complete` event is appended to the JSONL log. `config.status` is set to `"complete"` via `step_update_config_status`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1507:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/context-report-assembly.md:66:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/05--context-report-synthesis/context-report-assembly.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies all seven sections present in context-report.md, context-report.json parseable, synthesis_complete event in JSONL |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1514:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/scripts/README.md:133:| Registry is empty | No seat findings exist under `seats/` | Verify that at least one iteration completed and seats wrote their output JSON files |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1516:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/02--by-model-parallel-sweep/per-model-prompt-framework.md:22:Every seat in the heterogeneous pool receives a prompt built from the same four-part lineage contract, but the wrapper framework differs by model. A seat told only "analyze" returns generic noise; the correct framework channels each model's strengths toward the structured finding schema the host expects. Per-model framing is resolved via `sk-prompt-small-model` before any seat is dispatched.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1517:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/02--by-model-parallel-sweep/per-model-prompt-framework.md:34:4. **output schema** — the exact structured finding JSON the host expects back
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1519:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:9:> **EXECUTION POLICY**: Every scenario MUST be executed for real — not mocked, not stubbed, not classified as "unautomatable". AI agents executing these scenarios must run the actual commands, inspect real files, call real handlers, and verify real outputs. The only acceptable classifications are PASS, FAIL, or SKIP (with a specific sandbox blocker documented). "UNAUTOMATABLE" is not a valid status.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1524:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:92:4. Evidence is complete and readable.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1525:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:97:- `PARTIAL`: core behavior works but non-critical evidence or metadata is incomplete
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1526:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:116:4. No unresolved blocking triage item remains.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1528:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:164:Verify that the auto YAML's `phase_init` classifies session state (fresh / resume / completed-session / invalid) before writing any files, and that a fresh session creates all canonical state artifacts from the shipped templates.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1530:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:169:Expected signals: `step_classify_session` is named in the auto YAML; fresh/resume/completed-session/invalid outcomes are documented in `context.md` or SKILL.md; asset templates exist at `assets/deep_context_config.json`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1533:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:186:Expected signals: `step_seed_frontier` appears in the auto YAML; `code_graph_query` is the seeding tool in SKILL.md §3 and loop_protocol.md §4; Glob+Grep fallback is documented; strategy.md receives the frontier before `phase_loop`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1539:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:256:Prompt: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate the CLI council seats dispatch contract for `deep-context` against the auto YAML, loop_protocol.md, and SKILL.md §4 ALWAYS rules. Verify cli-opencode seats use `</dev/null` for closed stdin and omit top-level `--agent`; cli-codex seats use `--sandbox read-only`; and `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` provides `dispatchCouncilSeats`. Return a concise verdict.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1545:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:325:Verify that contradictions between seats (incompatible signatures or reuse verbs for the same `unit_id`) are detected, recorded as `CONTRADICTS` pairs, and surfaced in the findings registry — never silently resolved.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1547:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:330:Expected signals: `detectContradictions` function exported from `reduce-state.cjs`; `contradictions` key in registry; dashboard section "CONTRADICTIONS (surfaced, never auto-resolved)" exists in `renderDashboard`; loop_protocol.md §6 states contradictions are never silently resolved.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1561:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:440:Expected signals: `context_report_template.md` contains all seven section headings; `step_emit_report_json` or equivalent is named in the auto YAML; `phase_synthesis` updates `config.status = "complete"` via `step_update_config_status`; SKILL.md §3 describes both `context-report.md` and `context-report.json` as outputs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1583:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:16:Runtime template copied into the resolved `{artifact_dir}/` during initialization. Tracks review progress across iterations.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1584:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:27:- **Per iteration:** Agent reads Next Focus, reviews the assigned dimension/files, updates findings, marks dimensions complete, and sets new Next Focus.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1586:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:61:[None yet -- populated as iterations complete dimension reviews]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1587:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:83:[First iteration -- populated after iteration 1 completes]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1588:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:89:[First iteration -- populated after iteration 1 completes]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1589:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:128:[Alignment checks completed across core and overlay protocols]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1590:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:148:| [path/to/file] | [D1, D3] | [N] | [0 P0, 1 P1, 2 P2] | [partial/complete] |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1591:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_strategy.md:181:- Success criteria: zero P0 in correctness; test-coverage P0 resolved or deferred with rationale
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1592:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md:3:description: "loop-lock.cjs wraps the runtime acquireLoopLock/refreshLoopLock/releaseLoopLock helpers to provide single-writer advisory locking for the context loop. Auto and confirm YAMLs invoke it at step_acquire_lock and step_release_lock."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1593:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md:23:Provides best-effort single-writer advisory locking for the context loop: a second session targeting the same spec folder should find the lock held and fail closed at acquire. It is advisory, not a hard mutex — the host-driven loop runs as discrete `node` invocations rather than one long-lived process, so the owner PID is short-lived and a later run can reclaim a lock that stale-detection judges dead. The lock is acquired at the start of the YAML workflow and released on every exit path (synthesis, halt, cancel, workflow-exit).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1594:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md:45:### YAML Integration
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1595:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md:50:- **`step_release_lock`** (phase_synthesis, after synthesis completes) — `release` action; idempotent even if already released.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1596:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md:51:- Lock release is also wired into the halt, cancel, and completed-session exit paths in both YAMLs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1597:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md:55:The lock is a JSON file-based advisory lock, not a kernel fcntl lock. macOS/BSD locking is advisory: stale-lock override requires confirm-only or explicit recovery. The note appears in the YAML `step_acquire_lock` field.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1598:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md:74:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/07--runtime-robustness/loop-lock.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies `loop-lock.cjs` syntax; confirms `step_acquire_lock`/`step_release_lock` references exist in both YAML files |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1599:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deltas/iter-001.jsonl:1:{"type":"iteration","id":"R155-F001","severity":"P1","dimension":"correctness","title":"002 decision-record continuity still says R5 work remains","file":".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md","loc":"15-27","evidence":"Frontmatter says `next_safe_action: \"Run remaining R5 validation gates\"`, `completion_pct: 85`, and open question `Which R5 gates remain after R3/R4 close?`, while the same packet's plan/spec/checklist/summary all say R5 is done and only optional live-loop e2e remains.","impact":"Resume/memory consumers can revive a completed R5 closeout as still active, contradicting the packet's closure state.","recommendation":"Align this frontmatter with the other 002 docs: completion_pct 95, no open R5 question, and next_safe_action limited to optional live-loop e2e/metadata refresh.","iter":1,"timestamp":"2026-06-28T13:44:20.055Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1601:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/assets/agent_improvement/improvement_charter.md:35:Build a trustworthy improvement loop for agent surfaces by proving evaluation discipline before allowing self-editing. Evaluate agents holistically across their full integration surface, not just the prompt file, but the complete system of mirrors, commands, YAML workflows, skills, and gate routing that together define agent behavior.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1604:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/assets/agent_improvement/improvement_charter.md:81:| Benchmark completion | `benchmark_completed` | candidateId, aggregateScore, fixtureResults |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1605:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:3:description: "Classifies the context session (fresh, resume, restart, invalid) and creates all canonical state files before the first parallel sweep runs."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1606:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:22:Initialization is the workflow-owned entry into a context session. It reads existing state artifacts to decide whether the run is fresh, a resume, a restart, or an invalid state before writing anything. Fresh runs create `deep-context-config.json`, `deep-context-state.jsonl`, `deep-context-strategy.md`, and `findings-registry.json`. Resume detects complete, consistent prior state and appends a typed `resumed` event, then skips directly to `phase_loop`. Restart archives the current packet under the local `context_archive/` tree, mints a fresh `sessionId`, and increments `generation`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1607:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:34:- **completed-session**: artifacts exist and `config.status == "complete"`; halt with a message to archive or replace before a new session
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1608:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:35:- **invalid-state**: any partial, missing, or contradictory combination; halt for repair
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1609:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:39:Fresh runs call `step_create_config`, `step_create_state_log`, `step_create_strategy`, and `step_create_registry` in sequence. The config is written from the `deep_context_config.json` template with all resolved setup bindings. The JSONL log is seeded with one typed config record. The strategy file is populated with the seeded frontier, executor pool roster, and known context from memory. The findings registry is initialized with empty buckets and zeroed metrics.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1610:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:55:| `.opencode/commands/deep/context.md` | Command | Setup phase that resolves scope, spec_folder, executor_pool before YAML loads |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1611:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json:33:      "completed-continue"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1612:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/reuse-catalog-generation.md:46:The catalog ships `file:symbol` + signature references, never pasted source bodies. Downstream consumers pull bodies just-in-time when needed. This keeps the Context Report portable and avoids context rot from stale copy-pasted source.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1613:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/reuse-catalog-generation.md:64:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/05--context-report-synthesis/reuse-catalog-generation.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies REUSE catalog leads the report, all entries have verified freshness labels, and agreement counts match JSONL records |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1614:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/reuse-catalog-generation.md:76:- [reduce-state-merge.md](reduce-state-merge.md) — Registry refresh that keeps reuseCandidates current before compilation
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1615:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/02--by-model-parallel-sweep/native-task-batch.md:30:The critical constraint is that all native seats must be dispatched simultaneously in one parallel batch — not in a sequential for-each loop. Emitting Task calls one after another would serialize the sweeps and eliminate the concurrency benefit. The auto YAML enforces this with a `dispatch_mode: parallel_batch` directive.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1616:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/02--by-model-parallel-sweep/native-task-batch.md:38:4. `output schema` — the structured finding JSON the host expects
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1617:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/02--by-model-parallel-sweep/native-task-batch.md:55:| `.opencode/agents/deep-context.md` | Agent | Full LEAF read-only analyzer contract: input validation, BINDING lines, analysis workflow, output schema, anti-patterns |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1618:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md:24:Enforcement lives at the actual subprocess spawn site, `fanout-run.cjs`: it both stamps the stack via `buildExecutorDispatchEnv` and, before spawning, fails closed via `detectSameKindFromStack` when this kind is already on the inherited stack. The runtime `executor-audit.ts` module also provides a complete four-layer guard (`validateExecutorDispatchAllowed`: stack, ancestry, runtime-env, lockfile) used by the in-process dispatch helpers that already know they are a dispatched seat. `fanout-run.cjs` deliberately checks only the **stack** layer, because the orchestrator legitimately runs inside one of these runtimes — so the runtime-env (e.g. `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`) and ancestry layers would false-positive on the first-level dispatch. The YAML `cli_contract` documents the requirement.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1619:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md:30:### YAML Integration
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1620:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md:45:`validateExecutorDispatchAllowed(config, context)` checks four layers in order:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1621:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md:51:When blocked, `emitDispatchFailure` writes a `dispatch_failure` event to the JSONL state log with the recursion reason.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1622:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md:55:`buildExecutorAuditRecord` attaches `{ kind, model, reasoningEffort, serviceTier, lineageId? }` to the JSONL iteration record so every seat's provenance is traceable in the state log.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1623:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md:67:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts` | Shared | `buildExecutorDispatchEnv`, `validateExecutorDispatchAllowed`, `detectSameKindFromStack`, `detectFromAncestry`, `detectFromRuntimeEnv`, `detectFromLockfile`, `buildExecutorAuditRecord`, `emitDispatchFailure`, `CLI_DISPATCH_STACK_ENV` |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1624:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md:73:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/07--runtime-robustness/executor-audit.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies `SPECKIT_CLI_DISPATCH_STACK` and `executor-audit` references exist in the auto YAML |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1625:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/security-sensitive-fix-overrides.md:9:  - "fix completeness replay"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1626:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/security-sensitive-fix-overrides.md:25:This is a SPEC-ONLY contract. As of the current release, the runtime does NOT auto-detect security sensitivity and does NOT apply these overrides. `requiredClosedFindingReplay` and `requiredFixCompletenessGate` exist in no config, YAML, or reducer surface (verified by grep). Operators running a security-sensitive review must manually tighten thresholds via `--convergence` and `--max-iterations` and manually maintain the closed-gate replay table.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1627:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deltas/iter-003.jsonl:1:{"type":"iteration","id":"R155-F004","severity":"P1","dimension":"traceability","title":"ADR-004 claims 001 carry-forward was updated, but 001 still marks it pending","file":".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md","loc":"340","evidence":"002 decision-record.md:340 says \"Phase 001's carry-forward note is updated to point here as the resolution.\" But 001 tasks.md:99 still has unchecked \"NFR-S01 carried to 002 is closed or explicitly accepted there\", and 001 implementation-summary.md:102 still says \"NFR-S01 unresolved here.\"","impact":"ADR-004's accepted union-grant status is not traceable back through the 001 cross-refs; resume/checklist readers can still see the carry-forward as unresolved even though 002 records it as accepted.","recommendation":"Either update the 001 cross-refs/tasks/summary to say NFR-S01 is resolved by 002 ADR-004, or soften the ADR-004 implementation bullet so it does not claim the 001 carry-forward notes were reconciled.","iter":3,"timestamp":"2026-06-28T13:50:39.092Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1633:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-research/assets/deep_research_dashboard.md:58:- resolvedQuestions: [from findings registry metrics]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1639:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/01--frontier-seeding/config-shape-and-default-pool.md:77:| `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_context_auto.yaml` | `step_create_config`: writes the config from the resolved setup bindings |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1640:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/convergence-signals.md:21:These two signals catch a failure mode the severity-weighted composite vote can miss: a review that keeps producing moderate `newFindingsRatio` from reworded refinements while the genuinely new defect categories are already exhausted. They feed the legal-stop gate evaluation rather than the composite stop-score.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1641:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/convergence-signals.md:27:`semanticNovelty` (0.0 to 1.0) measures how much conceptually new review insight an iteration contributes beyond overlap with prior findings, independent of severity weighting. It is computed as new defect patterns over total patterns in the current iteration. When it drops below 0.15 for 2 or more consecutive evidence iterations, it provides strong supporting evidence for a legal STOP. When this `semanticNoveltyPlateau` sub-check fails while the existing churn signals pass, the gate records the mismatch as a diagnostic note but does not independently block STOP.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1644:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:58:      checks: Validate spec alignment, completeness, checklist evidence, and cross-reference integrity across linked artifacts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1645:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:103:        - evidence-completeness
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1646:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:157:    - id: completed-continue
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1647:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:159:      description: Reserved compatibility branch. The current runtime does not reopen completed sessions under a completed-continue lineage.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1649:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:186:      description: Review is ongoing or coverage is incomplete.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1650:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:190:      description: At least one unresolved P0 finding is present.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1653:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:233:      partialCriteria: Catalog coverage is incomplete or stale, but no confirmed contradiction to shipped behavior is present.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1654:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:261:        - resolvedFindings
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1655:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/review_mode_contract.yaml:283:        - completed-dimensions
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1659:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/reduce-state-merge.md:22:The reducer is the host-owned consolidation step that keeps `findings-registry.json` and `deep-context-dashboard.md` synchronized with the latest iteration trail. It applies the same relevance gate and agreement logic as `step_merge_findings`, so the registry is consistent whether the host is running inline or delegating to a standalone reducer pass.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1660:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/reduce-state-merge.md:53:After updating the registry, the reducer regenerates `deep-context-dashboard.md` from the JSONL log + registry data. The dashboard is auto-generated and must never be hand-edited.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1661:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/reduce-state-merge.md:57:During the loop, `step_update_registry` performs an equivalent in-loop merge after each iteration without invoking the script directly. `reduce-state.cjs` is primarily used as a standalone tool for recovery (reconstructing the registry from JSONL after a partial run) or post-run analysis.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1662:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/05--context-report-synthesis/reduce-state-merge.md:67:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Script | Full reducer: reads JSONL + seat files, computes agreement-weighted registry, writes dashboard |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1663:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/scope-binding-and-code-graph-seeding.md:22:This is the first meaningful computation after initialization. The scope string is never passed raw to executors — it is first resolved to a bounded set of `SLICE` nodes that define the iteration's shared focus. This keeps each parallel sweep tractable and prevents whole-repo noise from masking the high-signal code that actually matters for the feature.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1664:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/scope-binding-and-code-graph-seeding.md:38:When `code_graph_query` is unavailable or returns no results (stale graph), `step_seed_frontier` falls back to Glob + Grep over the anchor terms. Findings from the fallback path are tagged `frontier_source: "glob_grep_fallback"` in the `frontier_seeded` JSONL event, and downstream citations against these anchors are labeled `freshness: unverified` until the graph comes back online.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1665:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/scope-binding-and-code-graph-seeding.md:42:The ranked SLICE list is written into `deep-context-strategy.md` under the "Next Focus" section as the iteration-1 focus candidates. The count is logged in a `frontier_seeded` JSONL event. The frontier is never the whole repo — it is always a tractable slice set bounded by the scope anchors.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1670:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:119: * Parse JSONL content and report malformed lines (fail-closed pathway).

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1734:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/jsonl-repair.md:2:title: "JSONL Repair"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1735:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/jsonl-repair.md:15:# JSONL Repair
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1736:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/jsonl-repair.md:21:Before reading the append-only state log, `reduce-state.cjs` repairs any trailing malformed content caused by a mid-write crash, ensuring that the reducer always operates on valid JSONL.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1737:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/jsonl-repair.md:23:`repairJsonlTail` scans the state log from the beginning, keeping all complete valid JSON lines, and truncates anything after the last valid record. The repair outcome (`repaired`, `droppedBytes`) is captured in `registry.stateLogRepair` so operators know whether and how much data was trimmed.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1738:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/jsonl-repair.md:47:- Calls `validPrefixByteLength(content)` to find the byte offset of the last valid newline-terminated JSON record
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1739:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/jsonl-repair.md:48:- If `droppedBytes > 0`, truncates the file with `truncateSync(path, keepBytes)`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1740:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/02--by-model-parallel-sweep/heterogeneous-pool-dispatch.md:34:`step_sweep_native_batch` is skipped when no native seats exist in `config.fanout.executors`. When native seats are present, it emits all `@deep-context` Task dispatches in a single concurrent batch — explicitly not a sequential for-each loop. Each native seat receives the same rendered four-part prompt from `{prompt_dir}/iter-{NNN}/{label}.md`. Seats are read-only analyzers that return structured finding JSON to the host; the host writes each result to `{seat_dir}/iter-{NNN}/{label}.json`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1741:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/02--by-model-parallel-sweep/heterogeneous-pool-dispatch.md:38:`step_sweep_cli_pool` uses `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs#dispatchCouncilSeats` to fan all CLI seats out with `Promise.all`. Each CLI seat makes exactly one read-only analysis call — not an autonomous loop. A CLI seat's one-shot prompt carries the same four-part lineage contract (gather-subject, shared current_focus, known-context, seat_output_schema) and instructs the seat to return only the structured finding JSON for this single pass.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1742:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deltas/iter-005.jsonl:1:{"type":"iteration","id":"R155-F006","severity":"P1","dimension":"security","title":"ADR-004 accepts a hub union grant that is not actually the union of mode tool contracts","file":".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md","loc":"line 311","evidence":"ADR-004 says: \"Its `allowed-tools` grant is the union of the tools its nested modes require, including `WebFetch` for deep research.\" The live hub only grants `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md:5` `allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task, WebFetch]`, while mode packets require additional tools: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-research/SKILL.md:4` includes `memory_context, memory_search`, and `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/SKILL.md:4` includes `memory_context, memory_search, code_graph_query`.","impact":"NFR-S01 is closed on a permission-contract premise that does not match the repo. If hub tools govern `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)`, research/review may lose mode-required memory/code-graph tools; if mode frontmatter extends grants after dispatch, ADR-004's statement that the hub grant is the union is false and the accepted residual risk is incomplete.","recommendation":"Reconcile the contract: either make the hub `allowed-tools` the real full union and document the broader residual risk, or amend ADR-004/spec/checklist to say the hub is a partial routing grant and cite evidence that selected mode packet grants are applied after dispatch.","iter":5,"timestamp":"2026-06-28T13:58:54.842Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1744:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:15:This scenario validates Frontier Initialization for `FS-001`. It focuses on session classification logic (fresh / resume / completed-session / invalid) and canonical state file creation before the first parallel sweep runs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1745:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:19:The initialization phase is the single point where an incorrect session classification can corrupt an entire context-gathering run. Misclassifying a completed session as fresh deletes prior findings; misclassifying an invalid state as resumable causes the loop to run against corrupted state. Getting this gate right protects both continuity and data integrity.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1747:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:31:- Expected signals: `step_classify_session` is present in the auto YAML; fresh/resume/completed-session/invalid outcomes are documented in the command or SKILL.md; `assets/deep_context_config.json` exists; `step_acquire_lock` is present in the YAML init phase.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1749:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/01--frontier-seeding/frontier-initialization.md:49:| FS-001 | Frontier Initialization | Verify session classification gates file writes | `Validate that the deep-context initialization classifies session state correctly and only writes files on a fresh run.` | 1. `rg "step_classify_session" .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_context_auto.yaml` -> 2. `rg "fresh\|resume\|completed-session\|invalid" .opencode/commands/deep/context.md` -> 3. `ls .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/assets/deep_context_config.json` -> 4. `rg "step_acquire_lock" .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_context_auto.yaml` | Step 1: `step_classify_session` found in YAML; Step 2: all four session outcomes mentioned; Step 3: template file exists; Step 4: lock step found | Grep outputs from all four commands | PASS if steps 1-4 all return matches; FAIL if any step returns no output | 1. Check YAML path is correct. 2. Search for alternative session-classification wording in the YAML. 3. Confirm `deep_context_auto.yaml` is the current YAML filename. |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1769:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/assets/deep_review_dashboard.md:144:- 1 P0 in test-coverage dimension -- review cannot terminate PASS until resolved
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1770:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/03--agreement-merge/contradiction-surfacing.md:41:Contradictions are never silently resolved in the merge layer. When the host cannot reconcile from evidence (e.g. both seats cite different file:lines that both verify), the contradiction is surfaced as-is. The `@deep-context` agent is expected to surface contradictions in its finding set rather than picking one side (per its ESCALATE rule 4).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1771:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:3:description: "Decision record for deep-loop alignment. ADR-001 is accepted/executed for the deep-ai-council rename; ADR-002 keeps the merged-identity layer by sign-off with drift-guard green; ADR-003 keeps all five feature catalogs as earned; ADR-004 accepts the NFR-S01 hub union-grant contract."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1774:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:15:    recent_action: "ADR-001 done; ADR-002 kept; ADR-003 keep-all earned; ADR-004 union-grant accepted"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1775:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:16:    next_safe_action: "Run remaining R5 validation gates"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1776:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:29:      - "ADR-001 executed: folder/name identity is deep-ai-council."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1777:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:30:      - "ADR-002 accepted: keep the merged-identity layer by maintainer sign-off; the routing-registry drift-guard is green."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1778:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:31:      - "ADR-003 accepted: keep all five feature catalogs because each mode's catalog is substantial and earned."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1779:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:32:      - "ADR-004 accepted: NFR-S01 is a hub union-grant pattern; do not strip WebFetch in this pass."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1781:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:58:The former `ai-council` packet had folder `ai-council` but `SKILL.md` name `deep-ai-council`. That mismatch is now resolved on disk: the packet folder and SKILL name are both `deep-ai-council`. The `/deep:ai-council` command and `ai-council` agent remain legacy public surfaces, not filesystem identity.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1782:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:66:**We chose and executed**: rename the folder `ai-council` to `deep-ai-council` so `name == folder == deep-ai-council`, matching the `deep-<mode>` convention. The alternative, renaming the packet to `ai-council`, would break the convention and the established `deep-ai-council` identity the advisor and agents use.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1783:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:76:| **Rename folder → `deep-ai-council`** | `name == folder`; matches `deep-<mode>`; keeps the established `deep-ai-council` identity | Requires a one-pass reference rewire | CHOSEN |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1786:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:86:- Filesystem load paths use `deep-ai-council`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1789:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:98:| 1 | **Necessary?** | PASS | The family `--check` is red until the mismatch is resolved; R5 depends on it |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1791:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:129:| **Status** | Accepted - keep the merged-identity layer (sign-off; drift-guard green) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1793:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:146:**Keep the merged-identity layer.** Maintainer sign-off closes the decision, and the routing-registry drift-guard is green. Option E solves invocation through the hub, but it does not replace the advisor's stronger per-mode routing projection. Fixture comparison is optional hardening, not a prerequisite for this accepted keep decision.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1794:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:156:| **Keep the merged-identity layer** | Retains stronger per-mode advisor routing; matches deep-loop's heavy use; drift-guard is green | A drift-guarded Python+TS layer to maintain | CHOSEN |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1795:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:157:| **Simplify to hub-only identity** | Removes the drift-guarded layer; matches sk-design | Risk of per-mode routing regression; loses deep-loop's per-mode routing strength | Rejected |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1798:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:180:| 3 | **Sufficient?** | PASS | Maintainer sign-off plus a green drift-guard settles keep vs simplify for this pass |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1800:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:193:- Stage 4 is closed by maintainer sign-off: keep the merged-identity layer.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1803:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:219:All five deep modes carry a `feature_catalog/`. The sk-design conversion kept `feature_catalog/` only for the one mode whose feature set genuinely warranted the cataloging structure and removed thin catalogs as bloat. The same earned-keep test has now been applied to deep-loop, and the result differs because each deep-loop catalog is substantial: every mode has a version-stamped canonical inventory for a full autonomous-loop engine.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1804:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:227:Apply the same earned-keep test per deep mode and **keep all five** `feature_catalog/` directories. The earlier "remove the rest as bloat" premise was wrong for deep-loop: sk-design pruned thin modes, while deep-loop's five catalogs are all substantial and warranted. No deletion or reference repointing is needed.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:238:| **Keep all five `feature_catalog/`** | Preserves substantial version-stamped inventories; no dangling-reference risk | Keeps a larger doc surface | CHOSEN outcome of the earned-keep test |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1808:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:250:- The remaining validation work is R5, not catalog removal.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1813:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:285:- Stage 2 is closed as keep-all earned.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1815:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:311:The deep-loop hub is a routing-only parent skill. Its `allowed-tools` grant is the union of the tools its nested modes require, including `WebFetch` for deep research. Each mode still owns its operational tool contract in its own frontmatter. The runtime has not been proven to narrow the hub grant to the selected mode after dispatch, so the docs must not imply per-mode narrowing.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1816:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:319:Accept the union-grant contract for NFR-S01. The hub `allowed-tools` remains the union its modes need, the hub remains routing-only, and per-mode frontmatter remains the real mode contract. Do **not** strip `WebFetch` from the hub in this pass.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1817:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:328:- The residual risk is accepted for this packet because the hub is a dispatcher, not the execution policy for every mode.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1819:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/atomic-state.md:23:`reduce-state.cjs` uses two complementary mechanisms: for JSON state it delegates to `writeStateAtomic` from `atomic-state.ts` (loaded via `loadStateSafety`); for the markdown dashboard it applies an identical `writeTextAtomic` implemented inline. This dual-path design ensures atomic writes even when the TypeScript runtime toolchain is unavailable.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1820:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/07--runtime-robustness/atomic-state.md:40:1. `stateSafety.writeStateAtomic(registryPath, registry)` — atomic JSON write for `findings-registry.json`

R2 is done: the nested packet folder is `deep-ai-council`, matching the packet name, while `/deep:ai-council` and the `ai-council` agent remain legacy public surfaces. R3 is done as keep-all earned: all five mode catalogs are substantial and stay. R4 is done as keep: maintainer sign-off plus a green drift-guard keeps the merged-identity layer. NFR-S01 is resolved as an accepted hub union-grant permission contract. R5 is done: strict recursive spec validation, family package checks, routing fixtures, parent-skill invariants, and runtime-registration reachability are green. A full live-loop e2e was deliberately not run and remains optional.

R5 is done: strict recursive validation, package checks, routing fixtures, parent-skill invariants, and runtime-registration reachability are green. The full live-loop e2e remains optional and was not run.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:1947:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:78:- [x] All required gates green (`--check`, advisor/graph consistency by drift-guard + parity + parent-skill-check, routing fixtures, `validate.sh`)

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2002:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:325:**Decision**: Rename the folder to `deep-ai-council`; executed. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2003:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:332:- Renaming the packet to `ai-council`: rejected because it breaks the `deep-<mode>` convention and the established `deep-ai-council` identity the advisor and agents already use.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2005:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:336:**Status**: Accepted - keep the merged-identity layer (sign-off; drift-guard green)

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2009:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:350:**Decision**: Accept the hub `allowed-tools` union as the permission contract for the routing-only hub. Per-mode contracts remain mode-owned, residual hub grant risk is documented, and a runtime tool-narrowing probe is optional future hardening.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2010:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/03--agreement-merge/finding-dedup-by-symbol.md:53:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Script | Standalone reducer: same dedup + attribution + agreement logic over JSONL + seat files |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2011:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/04--convergence-detection/agreement-gate.md:30:`config.agreementMin` (default 2) is the minimum distinct-executor count for a finding to be classified as agreement-eligible. This value comes from the setup phase and is written into `config.fanout.executors` and the JSONL config record at initialization.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2012:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/04--convergence-detection/agreement-gate.md:34:Only agreement-eligible findings contribute to `new_agreement_eligible_count` in the per-iteration JSONL record. The saturation check uses `new_agreement_eligible_count / max(1, merged_findings_count)` as the convergence ratio — meaning single-executor findings do not advance convergence, no matter how many there are.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2016:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/severity-classification.md:21:The severity system converts raw review observations into blocker, required, or suggestion findings. Those levels control convergence math, verdict routing, and whether a finding can block release.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2017:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/feature_catalog/04--severity-system/severity-classification.md:27:The semantic meaning of each level is stable across the skill. P0 covers correctness failures, security vulnerabilities, and hard contradictions. P1 covers degraded behavior, incomplete implementation, or missing validation. P2 covers advisory items such as clarity gaps, naming issues, or documentation cleanup.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2018:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deltas/iter-004.jsonl:1:{"type":"iteration","id":"R155-F005","severity":"P1","dimension":"traceability","title":"Fix-completeness checklist claims SHA/diff pinning but the scoped packet docs contain no pin","file":".opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/checklist.md","loc":"104","evidence":"checklist.md:104 says '- [x] CHK-FIX-007 [P1] Evidence is pinned to a fix SHA or explicit diff range, not a moving branch-relative range.' Scoped rg over the requested packet docs for 'fix SHA', 'explicit diff range', 'git diff', 'commit', and commit-like hashes found only this checkbox plus tasks.md:63's 'baseline SHA' placeholder and plan.md's 'prior remediation evidence' references, with no actual SHA/range recorded.","impact":"R5/fix-completeness is marked done, but a reviewer cannot trace the claimed evidence set to a stable commit or diff range from the canonical scoped docs. That makes the closure branch-dependent despite the checklist saying it is pinned.","recommendation":"Record the actual baseline SHA and fix SHA or explicit diff range in the 002 docs, or replace the checked item with a precise pointer to the canonical evidence artifact. Otherwise uncheck or soften CHK-FIX-007.","iter":4,"timestamp":"2026-06-28T13:54:46.375Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2027:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/references/guides/quick_reference.md:104:> **Session classification:** `fresh` | `resume` | `completed-session` | `invalid-state` (inspected from config + JSONL + strategy before any write). `:restart` archives the packet and starts a new lineage segment (fresh sessionId, generation + 1).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2028:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/references/guides/quick_reference.md:120:| Contradictions | Incompatible signatures/reuse verbs for one `unit_id` surfaced as `CONTRADICTS` — never auto-resolved |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2029:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/references/guides/quick_reference.md:159:| `dependencyCompleteness` | 0.10 | Weighted | DEPENDENCY nodes with a resolved edge / total |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2032:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:11:      "evidence": "Frontmatter says `next_safe_action: \"Run remaining R5 validation gates\"`, `completion_pct: 85`, and open question `Which R5 gates remain after R3/R4 close?`, while the same packet's plan/spec/checklist/summary all say R5 is done and only optional live-loop e2e remains.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2033:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:12:      "impact": "Resume/memory consumers can revive a completed R5 closeout as still active, contradicting the packet's closure state.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2034:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:35:      "evidence": "graph-metadata.json:38 says \"status\": \"in_progress\", while spec.md:17 says \"Phase map reconciled; 001 done and 002 R1-R5 complete with optional live-loop e2e not run\" and spec.md:54 says \"Complete: R1-R5 done; all required gates green; live-loop e2e optional/not run\".",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2035:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:47:      "evidence": "002 decision-record.md:340 says \"Phase 001's carry-forward note is updated to point here as the resolution.\" But 001 tasks.md:99 still has unchecked \"NFR-S01 carried to 002 is closed or explicitly accepted there\", and 001 implementation-summary.md:102 still says \"NFR-S01 unresolved here.\"",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2036:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:48:      "impact": "ADR-004's accepted union-grant status is not traceable back through the 001 cross-refs; resume/checklist readers can still see the carry-forward as unresolved even though 002 records it as accepted.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2037:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:49:      "recommendation": "Either update the 001 cross-refs/tasks/summary to say NFR-S01 is resolved by 002 ADR-004, or soften the ADR-004 implementation bullet so it does not claim the 001 carry-forward notes were reconciled.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2038:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:56:      "title": "Fix-completeness checklist claims SHA/diff pinning but the scoped packet docs contain no pin",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2039:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:60:      "impact": "R5/fix-completeness is marked done, but a reviewer cannot trace the claimed evidence set to a stable commit or diff range from the canonical scoped docs. That makes the closure branch-dependent despite the checklist saying it is pinned.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2040:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:72:      "impact": "NFR-S01 is closed on a permission-contract premise that does not match the repo. If hub tools govern `Skill(deep-loop-workflows)`, research/review may lose mode-required memory/code-graph tools; if mode frontmatter extends grants after dispatch, ADR-004's statement that the hub grant is the union is false and the accepted residual risk is incomplete.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2041:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json:73:      "recommendation": "Reconcile the contract: either make the hub `allowed-tools` the real full union and document the broader residual risk, or amend ADR-004/spec/checklist to say the hub is a partial routing grant and cite evidence that selected mode packet grants are applied after dispatch.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2042:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/04--convergence-detection/context-coverage-signals.md:40:`coverage-graph-signals.ts` exports `evaluateContext(ns: Namespace): ContextConvergenceSignals` which queries the SQLite `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` tables for the session's namespace and derives all five signals. A `blendedScore` combines them (weighted toward reuse-first) and is recorded as `graph_convergence_score` in the JSONL log.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2043:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/04--convergence-detection/context-coverage-signals.md:44:After each iteration, `step_write_iteration` appends a JSONL iteration record that includes all five signal values alongside the stop reason, seat counts, and sweep duration. These records are the source of truth for trending and stuck detection.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2044:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/04--convergence-detection/context-coverage-signals.md:62:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/04--convergence-detection/context-coverage-signals.md` | Manual playbook | Verifies all five signals are present in JSONL records and that convergence.cjs returns parseable JSON |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2045:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-context/feature_catalog/03--agreement-merge/cross-executor-agreement.md:38:`agreementRate` is computed as `count(agreement-eligible units) / count(all surviving units after relevance gate)`. This metric is persisted in the JSONL iteration record and in the coverage graph snapshot. `convergence.cjs` uses it as a blocking guard: `agreementRate < 0.50` → `STOP_BLOCKED`. `reduce-state.cjs` recomputes it in the same way when run as a standalone reducer.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2048:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/SKILL.md:21:Convergence threshold semantics and sibling-parity notes (deep-review 0.10 vs deep-research 0.05 vs deep-ai-council 0.20) live in `references/convergence/convergence.md` §1 under "Threshold Semantics and Sibling Parity".
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2051:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-review/SKILL.md:54:- Manage iteration state outside the resolved local review packet under `{spec_folder}/review/`
