The agent body uses §8 to tell the planning LEAF what report to return; `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs` uses this contract to parse that report and persist packet-local `ai-council/` artifacts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2951:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md:23:The report remains markdown, not JSON. The helper accepts normal heading variation, extracts the strict-required sections, and degrades gracefully for optional narrative sections.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2952:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md:27:- Agent body: `.opencode/agents/ai-council.md` §8 OUTPUT FORMAT and §16 Caller Persistence Protocol
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2953:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md:28:- Runtime mirrors: `.claude/agents/ai-council.md`, `.codex/agents/ai-council.toml`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2954:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md:29:- Helper: `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2955:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md:52:Strict-required means the helper must fail before writing any files. Optional means the helper can still produce a valid artifact tree.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2956:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/output_schema.md:114:- `ai-council-state.jsonl` records `new_findings_count` from extra unknown headings, not optional-section presence.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2963:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/skill-benchmark/browser-executor.cjs:90:// sandbox headless and assert the API exports + a completed animation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2968:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:3:description: "This scenario validates idempotent upsert behavior and strict-schema rejection of self-loop edges for `DAC-019`. It anchors to the council-graph-script.vitest.ts test 'upserts prompt-safe council graph data and queries unresolved disagreements and decision support'."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2969:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:15:This scenario validates that `runtime upsert CLI` is idempotent across repeated calls (same `(specFolder, sessionId, nodeId/edgeId)` → no duplicate rows) and that self-loop edges (where `from === to`) are rejected by the strict input schema before any storage write.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2970:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:19:Derived graph rows are replayable from `ai-council/**` artifacts. A reducer or caller will frequently re-upsert the same nodes during convergence loops; without idempotency, row counts drift and convergence math becomes meaningless. Self-loops corrupt graph traversal (`evidence_chain` and `unresolved_disagreements` queries depend on acyclic edges), so they must fail closed at the input boundary.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2971:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:29:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI twice with the same node payload and assert single-row state, then attempt a self-loop edge and assert strict-schema rejection.`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2972:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:30:- Expected execution process: Issue two runtime CLI `runtime upsert CLI` calls with identical `nodes` payloads; query node count via `runtime status CLI`; issue a third `runtime upsert CLI` with a `from === to` edge; assert validation error.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2973:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:31:- Expected signals: Second upsert reports no new rows (or matching `inserted: 0`); status node count equals first-call count; self-loop edge call returns input-validation error before any row write.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2974:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:46:6. Call `runtime upsert CLI` with an edge where `from === to`; capture the validation error response.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2975:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:50:`As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI twice with the same node payload and assert single-row state, then attempt a self-loop edge and assert strict-schema rejection.`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2976:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:62:Step 4 status counts equal step 2 status counts (idempotent). Step 5 returns a strict-schema validation error naming the self-loop constraint; no row written.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2977:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:66:Capture the four runtime CLI responses and the schema validation error message verbatim.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2978:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:70:- **Pass**: Step 4 counts equal step 2 counts; step 5 returns validation error; node/edge totals after step 5 unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2979:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:71:- **Fail**: Step 4 counts higher than step 2 (duplicate write); step 5 succeeds (self-loop accepted).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2980:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-idempotency-and-self-loop-rejection.md:75:If duplicates appear, inspect `lib/council/council-graph-db.ts` `upsertNodes`/`upsertEdges` for unique-constraint regression. If self-loop accepted, inspect `schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts` `councilGraphUpsertInputSchema` and `scripts/upsert.cjs` for validation drift.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3033:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:22:**Persistence is mandatory for every council run.** The target packet path is resolved by the agent at §1 Step 0 RESOLVE in `deep-ai-council.md` across all 4 runtime mirrors. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3034:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:24:The `ai-council/` folder is the packet-local artifact home for `@deep-ai-council` runs. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3039:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:48:`ai-council-config.json` is one mutable packet config. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3040:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:50:`ai-council-strategy.md` is the first-run charter. It records task framing, selected lenses, executor/vantage targets, input evidence, constraints, and the convergence rule.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3041:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:52:`ai-council-state.jsonl` is the append-only state log. It records `round_start`, `seat_returned`, `deliberation_synthesized`, `round_end`, `council_complete`, `artifact_written`, `rollback`, and `artifact_superseded` events for resume and audit.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3042:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:64:**Canonical writer:** the council writes these artifacts directly with `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/lib/persist-artifacts.cjs` named exports. Each writer is scoped to `ai-council/**` and appends an `artifact_written` audit event. `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs` remains a helper fallback for non-council callers.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3043:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/references/structure/folder_layout.md:69:- Agent body: `.opencode/agents/ai-council.md` §12 OUTPUT PROTOCOL (this layout) + §13 INVOCATION CONTRACT + §14 STATE SCHEMA + §16 COUNCIL PERSISTENCE PROTOCOL + §18 ROLLBACK FOR OPERATORS

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3051:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.2.0.0.md:7:This release aligns `deep-ai-council` with the shared deep-skill resource model while preserving its planning-only domain. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3067:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:3:description: "This scenario validates that `runtime upsert CLI` returns explicit no-op success on empty input rather than erroring. Anchors to council-graph-script.vitest.ts test 'treats empty upsert as an explicit no-op success'."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3068:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:15:This scenario validates that calling `runtime upsert CLI` with empty `nodes` and empty `edges` arrays returns explicit no-op success (the response indicates `inserted: 0` or equivalent `noop: true` flag) instead of either erroring or silently swallowing the input.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3069:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:29:- Prompt: `As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI with empty nodes and empty edges arrays and assert the response indicates explicit no-op success.`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3070:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:49:`As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI with empty nodes and empty edges arrays and assert the response indicates explicit no-op success.`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3071:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/manual_testing_playbook/08--council-graph-integration/council-graph-upsert-empty-input-no-op-success.md:76:| DAC-020 | runtime upsert CLI empty input no-op | Verify empty payload returns explicit no-op success | `As a council-graph integration validator, call runtime upsert CLI with empty nodes and empty edges arrays and assert the response indicates explicit no-op success.` | status -> upsert (empty) -> status | Explicit no-op response + unchanged counts | runtime CLI responses + status counts | PASS if no-op success and unchanged counts | Inspect scripts/upsert.cjs empty-input branch |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3073:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/lib/README.md:33:| Equals-only, bare flag is boolean | `score-candidate.cjs`, `promote-candidate.cjs`, `generate-profile.cjs`, `scan-integration.cjs`, `rollback-candidate.cjs`, `check-mirror-drift.cjs` | `--key=value` only | A bare `--key` is `true`. All docs and YAML invoke these with the equals form, so adding space-form would change parsing semantics (`--key value` would bind the value instead of staying boolean) for no caller benefit. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3074:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.3.0.0.md:1:deep-ai-council's fifteen reference files are now grouped into five topic subfolders, so a reader reaches convergence, scoring, structure, pattern, and integration docs by area instead of scanning one flat list.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3075:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.3.0.0.md:11:- **Grouped references/ into convergence/, scoring/, structure/, patterns/, and integration/** -- the fifteen references sat in one flat directory. They now live under five topic subfolders. The smart router discovers references recursively, so routing behavior is unchanged while navigation gets clearer.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3076:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v2.3.0.0.md:12:- **Updated the README structure tree and every inbound link** -- the SKILL.md REFERENCES paths and intent map, the README structure diagram, and the @ai-council agent mirrors across four runtimes resolve to the subfoldered paths. A workspace sweep confirmed zero stale flat reference paths in live consumers.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3077:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:1:First release of `deep-ai-council` as a dedicated skill. Council deliberation moves out of `system-spec-kit` and into its own package, the runtime agent is renamed from `multi-ai-council` to `deep-ai-council` across all three runtimes, the advisor learns to route council prompts to the new skill, and the planning-only boundary is enforced through scoped-write helpers that only ever touch packet-local `ai-council/**` artifacts. Two spec packets ship behind this release: 101/001 created the skill surface, 101/003 expanded references and tests to fully document the agent contract.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3078:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:3:> Spec folders: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/101-deep-multi-ai-council-skill/{001-deep-ai-council-skill-creation, 003-deep-ai-council-reference-expansion}` (Level 1 each)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3079:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:9:The headline change is that AI council deliberation now has a real home. Before this release, the council was a multi-strategy planning idea threaded through `system-spec-kit`, but the references, persistence scripts, and routing all lived inside an unrelated skill, which made it hard to evolve council behavior without touching spec-folder governance. This release lifts council out into its own package with its own runtime agent.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3080:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:15:The new `deep-ai-council` skill owns council activation triggers, smart routing across eight council intents, the planning-only operational boundary, and the rules that make council reports reproducible. The skill stays codebase-agnostic, so any project can dispatch a council without inheriting Webflow or OpenCode-specific patterns.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3081:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:21:The runtime agent now ships as `@deep-ai-council` across all three runtime mirrors (OpenCode, Claude, Codex). The agent body documents the 9-step workflow from RESOLVE through DELIVER, the dispatch depth rules (Depth 0 parallel via `Task`, Depth 1 sequential via `sequential_thinking` MCP), and the persistence protocol that calls writer functions in a fixed order so every artifact lands with an audit event.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3082:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:27:Natural prompts like "Run an AI council deliberation to compare implementation plans" used to fall through to a generic explainer because the source scorer's read-only guard tripped on phrases like "compare implementation plans". The advisor source scorer now allows explicit `deep-ai-council` council phrases through that guard, returning the new skill with confidence 0.95 and uncertainty 0.12 on the regression suite.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3083:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:33:The release adds a clean library boundary for everything the council writes to disk, and it preserves the additive-only state-event policy so existing council JSONL state stays readable.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3084:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:39:A new library at `scripts/lib/persist-artifacts.js` exposes seven named writer functions, one per artifact type (`writeConfig`, `writeStrategyMd`, `writeStateJsonl`, `writeSeat`, `writeDeliberation`, `writeCritique`, `writeReport`). Every writer resolves the destination under `<packet>/ai-council/`, refuses to write outside that root with an `OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE` error, then appends an `artifact_written` audit event with byte count and SHA-256 checksum to the append-only state log. The previous CLI wrapper at `scripts/persist-artifacts.cjs` is now a thin entry point that delegates to the library, so non-council callers still work unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3085:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:45:The state log keeps the v1 event types (round_start, seat_returned, deliberation_synthesized, round_end, council_complete) and adds v1.2 audit events (`artifact_written`, `rollback`, `artifact_superseded`) without breaking older readers. The protocol string stays `multi-ai-council` for compatibility with existing council state rows produced before the rename — the policy is additive only, never rewrite history.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3086:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:51:Failed rounds move into `ai-council/failed/round-NNN-<timestamp>/` with matching `artifact_superseded` markers in the state log. Operators can inspect the failed round folder for forensic context, then dispatch a fresh round; in-place retry is intentionally out of scope so the audit trail stays clean.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3087:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:63:Six references cover the day-to-day surface: `output_schema.md` (the markdown report contract the parser enforces), `folder_layout.md` (packet-local artifact tree), `state_format.md` (append-only JSONL events), `command_wiring.md` (caller-owned post-dispatch persistence), `seat_diversity_patterns.md` (lens combinations and vantage targets), and `convergence_signals.md` (two-of-three-agree rule and escape hatches). Four further references were added in 101/003 to lift agent-body logic into the reference layer: `scoring_rubric.md` documents the 5-dimension rubric, multi-round deliberation, and the Hunter / Skeptic / Referee adversarial cross-critique; `depth_dispatch.md` codifies Depth 0 vs Depth 1 detection and no-recursive-counciling rules; `failure_handling.md` walks through timeouts, all-seats-fail, contradiction-without-resolution, and insufficient vantage diversity; `anti_patterns.md` lists the eleven council anti-patterns with detection cues and recovery actions.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3088:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:75:The playbook follows the canonical `manual_testing_playbook.md` plus numbered category folders shape. Eighteen scenarios across seven categories (DAC-001 through DAC-018) cover runtime routing, council deliberation and seat diversity, artifact persistence and state format, convergence and rollback, scope boundaries, depth detection and resume semantics, and the writer library contract. Every per-feature file carries the required nine-column scenario table, realistic operator prompts, and source anchors back to the implementation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3089:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:81:Targeted vitest coverage already exists at `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/multi-ai-council-*.vitest.ts` and continues to pass after the rename, the persistence library refactor, and the sk-code style alignment.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:3094:.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-ai-council/changelog/v1.0.0.0.md:97:No new top-level commands ship in this release. The mechanism must not move per-mode workflow logic, convergence/state behavior, or semantic tool-use boundaries into the hub, and any effective permission widening caused by Skill(<parent>) must be explicit in the decision record. A stricter per-mode runtime sandbox is a separate runtime enhancement, not part of Option E.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/001-invocability-mechanism/spec.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-5.md:49:- S15: Add this exact 001 decision-record wording after ADR-001 `Why this one`: `**Security / NFR-S01 evaluation**: Option E uses the parent hub's allowed-tools for parent invocation. That is an intentional parent-skill union grant, not proof that the runtime narrows to the selected packet's frontmatter. The hub stays routing-only; each mode packet retains its own behavior contract, but those contracts are not a separate runtime permission sandbox when reached through Skill(<parent>). For deep-loop, WebFetch must remain available to the family hub because the research mode declares and uses it. Therefore NFR-S01 is satisfied only as an explicit, documented union-grant contract. Runtime-enforced per-mode narrowing remains a future Option-D-style enhancement.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/001-invocability-mechanism/decision-record.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-5.md:50:- S16: Replace 002 NFR-S01 with exact wording: `- **NFR-S01**: Deep-loop alignment must preserve the parent-hub union-grant contract: the deep-loop-workflows hub may carry the union of tools needed by its modes, including WebFetch for deep-research. Alignment must not flatten per-mode workflow logic into the hub or change mode-owned instructions; inward-only modes remain inward-only by their packet contracts. The effective hub grant must be documented as broader than some mode frontmatters, and runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing is out of scope unless separately designed and verified.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-5.md:51:- S17: Add a new 002 decision-record section with exact wording: `## ADR-004: Parent-hub tool grant for NFR-S01\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Status** | Accepted - document union-grant contract |\n| **Date** | 2026-06-28 |\n\n### Context\n`deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md` is the invokable hub. Its allowed-tools includes WebFetch; `deep-research/SKILL.md` also includes WebFetch, while context/review/ai-council/improvement do not. The parent-skill hub template says hub allowed-tools is the union the modes need.\n\n### Decision\nKeep WebFetch on the deep-loop hub. Treat hub allowed-tools as the effective family-level grant for Skill(deep-loop-workflows); the hub remains routing-only and mode packets retain their own behavioral/tool-use contracts. Do not claim runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing unless a separate runtime enhancement proves it.\n\n### Consequences\n- This resolves NFR-S01 by documenting the effective contract instead of pretending the hub is an intersection grant.\n- Inward-only mode packets remain inward-only by instruction and review gates, not by a separate runtime permission sandbox when entered through the parent hub.\n- Removing WebFetch is rejected because it would break the deep-research route through the hub.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-5.md:52:- S18: Do not change live hub allowed-tools in this remediation pass; any runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing should be a separate high-blast design with tests. [.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md, .opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md, .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/skill/parent_skill_hub_template.md] risk=med
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-5.md:55:- Should remediation change live hub tool permissions to address NFR-S01, or reconcile docs to the current union-grant hub pattern? -> rec: Prefer doc/risk reconciliation for this R1/R2 remediation pass; live permission changes are higher-blast and should be a separately approved fix.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-5.md:58:- Should the four non-council SKILL.md files gain explicit feature_catalog pointers? -> rec: Yes, but treat it as a small live-doc edit after the packet-doc reconciliation, because only deep-ai-council currently exposes the catalog from SKILL.md.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-5.md:59:- Should R4 be closed as kept, or remain pending until a comparative hub-only routing benchmark is run? -> rec: Close as kept now; benchmark only if someone proposes simplification.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/assets/skill_benchmark/default_profile.json:12:  "notes": "Reference profile only — NOT yet consumed at runtime. The scorer hardcodes WEIGHTS in scripts/skill-benchmark/score-skill-benchmark.cjs and there is no --profile loader on the Lane C path yet; this weights block is the forward-looking source these will read once profile loading lands. Mode A (router-replay) scores D1-intra, D2, D3, D5 deterministically; D1-inter (advisor) is built but opt-in via --advisor-mode=python; D4 (usefulness ablation) requires live mode and is reported unscored until then. Weights/bands are provisional pending 2-3 pilot calibration per the 002 playbook."
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:9:> **EXECUTION POLICY**: Every scenario MUST be executed for real, not mocked, not stubbed, not classified as "unautomatable". AI agents executing these scenarios must run the actual commands, inspect real files, call real handlers, and verify real outputs. The only acceptable classifications are PASS, FAIL, or SKIP (with a specific sandbox blocker documented). "UNAUTOMATABLE" is not a valid status.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:33:This playbook provides deterministic scenarios across the categories listed in the canonical package artifacts above, validating the current `deep-improvement` skill surface from the core scoring and loop categories (01-07) through the agent-discipline stress tests (08), the Lane B model-benchmark scenarios (09), the Lane C skill-benchmark scenarios (10), and the Lane D non-dev-ai-system scenarios (11). Each scenario maps to a dedicated feature file with the canonical objective, prompt summary, expected signals, and command-specific evidence requirements.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:91:4. Evidence is complete, readable, and tied to the same run that produced the verdict.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:96:- `PARTIAL`: core behavior works but non-critical evidence or metadata is incomplete
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:112:4. No unresolved blocking triage item remains.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:113:5. Drift between root summaries and per-feature files has been resolved, with the per-feature file treated as the temporary source of truth until resynchronized.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:139:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that scanning a known agent discovers all integration surfaces and confirms mirror alignment against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify `status: "complete"` at root level. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:141:Expected signals: `status: "complete"` at root level; `surfaces.canonical.exists: true`; `surfaces.mirrors` array with 3 entries, each with `syncStatus: "aligned"`; `summary.totalSurfaces >= 20`; `summary.mirrorSyncStatus: "all-aligned"`; `summary.commandCount >= 1`; `summary.skillCount >= 1`; Exit code is 0
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:152:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that scanning a nonexistent agent produces graceful error handling instead of a crash against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify `status: "complete"` (exit code 0 -- the script completes gracefully, it does not crash). Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:154:Expected signals: `status: "complete"` (exit code 0 -- the script completes gracefully, it does not crash); `surfaces.canonical.exists: false`; All entries in `surfaces.mirrors` have `syncStatus: "missing"`; `summary.missingCount > 0`; No unhandled exception or stack trace
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:165:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that scanning the debug agent discovers a broad set of integration surfaces (20+ expected) against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify `status: "complete"`. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:167:Expected signals: `status: "complete"`; `summary.totalSurfaces >= 20`; `surfaces.mirrors` entries with `syncStatus: "aligned"`; `summary.commandCount >= 1`; `summary.skillCount >= 5`; Surfaces span commands, skills, and global docs (CLAUDE.md, agent definitions, skill routing entries); Exit code is 0
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:178:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that the --output flag writes scan results to a valid JSON file at the specified path against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify File `/tmp/test-scan-output.json` is created after the command completes. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:180:Expected signals: File `/tmp/test-scan-output.json` is created after the command completes; The file contains valid JSON (parseable without errors); JSON structure matches stdout output: `status`, `surfaces`, `summary` top-level fields; `surfaces.canonical.exists: true`, `surfaces.mirrors` array present; `summary.totalSurfaces`, `summary.mirrorSyncStatus` fields present
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:236:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that the --output flag writes a valid profile JSON file for the review agent against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify File `/tmp/test-profile.json` is created after the command completes. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:238:Expected signals: File `/tmp/test-profile.json` is created after the command completes; The file contains valid JSON (parseable without errors); JSON structure includes top-level fields: `id`, `derivedChecks`, `agentMeta`; `derivedChecks` contains `ruleCoherence` and `outputChecks` sub-fields; Exit code is 0
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:278:providing a nonexistent candidate file results in an infra_failure status and exit code 1.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:281:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that providing a nonexistent candidate file results in an infra_failure status and exit code 1 against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Exit code is 1 (not 0). Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:283:Expected signals: Exit code is 1 (not 0); Output is valid JSON (no stack trace); `status` field equals `"infra_failure"`; `failureModes` array contains `"profile-generation-failure"`; No unhandled exception or stack trace is printed
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:300:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that running a benchmark without the --integration-report flag produces output with no integration-specific fields against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Benchmark completes successfully with exit code 0. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:302:Expected signals: Benchmark completes successfully with exit code 0; Output JSON at `/tmp/bench-no-integration.json` is valid and contains:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:332:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate backward compatibility: JSONL records that lack dimension fields still produce a normal reducer dashboard against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Reducer completes without errors, exit code 0. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:334:Expected signals: Reducer completes without errors, exit code 0; Dashboard generated at `/tmp/reducer-test-nodim/agent-improvement-dashboard.md`; Registry generated at `/tmp/reducer-test-nodim/experiment-registry.json`; Dashboard does NOT contain a "Dimensional Progress" table (no dimension data in records); Standard composite score and verdict sections are present
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:345:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that JSONL records containing dimension data produce a Dimensional Progress table in the reducer output against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Reducer completes without errors, exit code 0. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:347:Expected signals: Reducer completes without errors, exit code 0; Dashboard generated at `/tmp/reducer-test-dim/agent-improvement-dashboard.md`; Dashboard includes a "Dimensional Progress" section with a table showing columns: Dimension, Latest, Best, Trend; All 5 dimensions appear in the table: `structural`, `ruleCoherence`, `integration`, `outputQuality`, `systemFitness`; Standard composite score and verdict sections are also present alongside the dimensional table
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:358:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that three or more consecutive identical dimension scores trigger the plateau stop condition against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Reducer completes without errors, exit code 0. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:360:Expected signals: Reducer completes without errors, exit code 0; Dashboard generated at `/tmp/reducer-test-plateau/agent-improvement-dashboard.md`; Registry generated at `/tmp/reducer-test-plateau/experiment-registry.json`; Registry shows `stopStatus.shouldStop: true` with reason mentioning "dimensions plateaued" or similar; Dimensional Progress table shows the flat score trend across all 3 records
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:377:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate the complete /deep:agent-improvement loop end-to-end using the debug agent as the target against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Init phase creates `improvement/` directory with config, charter, strategy, and manifest. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:379:Expected signals: Init phase creates `improvement/` directory with config, charter, strategy, and manifest; Integration scan runs and produces `integration-report.json`; Candidate generated under `improvement/candidates/`; Score output produced via dynamic-mode 5-dimension scoring; Dashboard generated at `improvement/agent-improvement-dashboard.md`; Loop completes 1 iteration without errors
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:390:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate the complete /deep:agent-improvement loop targeting a non-standard agent (debug.md) to confirm the pipeline is not hardcoded to specific agents against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Dynamic profile generated on-the-fly (debug.md has no static profile). Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:413:the improvement loop detects when a mutation improves one dimension at the cost of another, reports the trade-off explicitly, and does not auto-promote candidates with unresolved trade-offs.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:416:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that the improvement loop detects when a mutation improves one dimension at the cost of another, reports the trade-off explicitly, and does not auto-promote candidates with unresolved trade-offs against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Dimension trajectory tracked per iteration (at least 3 data points before convergence claim). Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:418:Expected signals: Dimension trajectory tracked per iteration (at least 3 data points before convergence claim); Trade-off detected when one dimension delta is positive and another is negative beyond threshold; Trade-off report includes: affected dimensions, magnitude of change, Pareto assessment; Journal emits `trade-off-detected` event with structured data; Candidate with unresolved trade-off is flagged for human review, not auto-promoted; Dashboard shows dimension trajectories with trade-off annotations
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:445:every completed improvement session emits a `session_ended` event with a valid `stopReason` and `sessionOutcome` drawn from the frozen taxonomies defined in `improvement-journal.cjs`.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:448:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that every completed improvement session emits a session_ended event with a valid stopReason and sessionOutcome drawn from the frozen taxonomies defined in improvement-journal.cjs against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify Journal contains at least one `session_ended` or `session_end` event. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:528:Expected signals: `improvement_config.json` has `parallelWaves.enabled: false` by default; `parallelWaves.maxCandidates: 3` (configured but not active); During an improvement session with defaults: only one candidate is generated per iteration; Candidate lineage (if tracked) shows all nodes with `waveIndex: 0` (single-wave); No parallel mutation spawning occurs regardless of exploration-breadth score; The activation conditions (exploration-breadth threshold, 3+ unresolved mutation families, 2 consecutive ties) are never evaluated when `enabled: false`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:539:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that the /deep:agent-improvement autonomous workflow wires improvement-journal.cjs at every required boundary: session_start, per-iteration lifecycle checkpoints, nested legal_stop_evaluated.details.gateResults, benchmark_completed, and session_end against the current deep-improvement command, runtime artifacts, and validation scripts. Verify `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_agent-improvement_auto.yaml` contains `improvement-journal.cjs` emission steps for:. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:541:Expected signals: `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_agent-improvement_auto.yaml` contains `improvement-journal.cjs` emission steps for session_start, candidate_generated, candidate_scored, benchmark_completed, nested legal_stop_evaluated.details.gateResults, and session_end:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:620:Prompt summary: Dispatch the same scorer-friendly fixture task twice. Call B must include `CRITIC PASS` notes covering `scorer overfit`, `helper bypass`, `mirror drift concealment`, `fixture narrowness`, and `promotion leakage`, while keeping the candidate packet-local.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:633:Confirm legal-stop gates are emitted as complete structured evidence and block convergence when any gate fails.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:667:Confirm benchmark execution emits `benchmark_completed` and writes a sentinel, not only repeatability output or action prose.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:671:Prompt summary: Dispatch the same benchmark-boundary fixture task twice. Call B must cite `run-benchmark.cjs`, create `/tmp/cp-037-sandbox/benchmark-completed.sentinel`, emit `benchmark_completed`, and avoid treating `benchmark-stability.cjs` alone as completion evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:673:Expected signals: B combined transcript/artifacts include `run-benchmark.cjs`, `benchmark_completed`, and `benchmark-completed.sentinel`; sentinel existence check exits 0; project tripwire is empty.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:678:> **Feature File:** [CP-037](08--agent-discipline-stress-tests/benchmark-completed-boundary.md)
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:692:Prompt summary: As a manual-testing orchestrator, validate that loop-host routes model-benchmark mode to materialize plus run-benchmark and keeps the agent-improvement default unchanged. Verify the model-benchmark route materializes fixtures and writes a `benchmark-complete` report while the default route stays on the agent-improvement scorer. Return a concise operator-facing PASS/FAIL verdict with the decisive evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:694:Expected signals: model-benchmark run completes with exit code 0; `materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs` emits `status: "fixtures-materialized"` before scoring; `run-benchmark.cjs` writes `report.json` with `status: "benchmark-complete"`; the appended `benchmark_run` row carries `mode: "model-benchmark"`; the default-route run routes to `score-candidate.cjs` and never loads `dispatch-model.cjs`; unknown mode warns and falls back to agent-improvement
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:707:Expected signals: Benchmark completes with exit code 0; `report.json` has `status: "benchmark-complete"`; `report.json` has `scoringMethod: "pattern"`; each `fixtures[]` entry has `missingHeadings`, `missingPatterns`, and `forbiddenMatches` arrays; NO `dimensions` object on any fixture entry; appended `benchmark_run` row carries `scoringMethod: "pattern"` and `mode: "model-benchmark"`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:720:Expected signals: Benchmark completes with exit code 0; `report.json` has `status: "benchmark-complete"`; `report.json` has `scoringMethod: "5dim"`; each `fixtures[]` entry has `scoringMethod: "5dim"` and a `dimensions` object with keys `D1`-`D5`; each dimension value is numeric in `0.0`-`1.0`; `D4` equals `1.0` under noop; appended `benchmark_run` row carries `scoringMethod: "5dim"` and `mode: "model-benchmark"`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:733:Expected signals: Unknown-scorer run completes with exit code 0; stderr contains `unknown --scorer 'bogus', defaulting to 'pattern'`; the unknown-scorer `report.json` has `scoringMethod: "pattern"`; unknown-mode run does not crash; stderr contains `unknown mode 'bogus', defaulting to 'agent-improvement'`; the unknown-mode run routes to `score-candidate.cjs` and produces a score JSON, not a benchmark report
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:778:Expected signals: the skill-benchmark run completes with exit code 0 and emits `skill-benchmark-report.json` plus the rendered `skill-benchmark-report.md`; `--mode=bogus` writes `unknown mode 'bogus', defaulting to 'agent-improvement'` to stderr; the default and model-benchmark routes are unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:804:Expected signals: two runs over the same task exit 0 and produce byte-identical JSON (deterministic); `parseable: true`; the resolved `intents` and `resources` reflect the task (e.g. a REVIEW task routes to the expected resources with no missing resources).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:838:the orchestrator emits a machine `skill-benchmark-report.json` and a human `skill-benchmark-report.md` rendered from it (anti-drift); ranked bottleneck classes are members of the remediation taxonomy.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:843:Expected signals: the run exits 0 and writes both `skill-benchmark-report.json` and `.md`; re-rendering via `build-report.cjs --report <json>` reproduces the orchestrator markdown byte-for-byte (anti-drift); every bottleneck `class` is a member of `assets/skill_benchmark/remediation_taxonomy.json` (classes carry `severity`, `oneLineFix`, and `handoffLane`). Note: the taxonomy is a reference asset validated by test; the report code does not yet enrich bottlenecks with its fields.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:867:The feature catalog root is `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md`. Use it as the current-state capability index when a scenario needs source-of-truth feature context beyond the command transcript.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-9.md:65:- Should remediation run a live runtime probe for 001 now? -> rec: A. The accepted mechanism is hub routing through an already invocable parent; running a runtime-extensibility probe would reintroduce obsolete work.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-9.md:68:- How should R3 close? -> rec: Keep R3 pending until a per-mode table is added; if every mode earns its catalog, close R3 as keep-all with evidence rather than as an assumed keep-all.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-9.md:69:- When can 002 move from in_progress to complete? -> rec: Use the first option. Package checks support the 65% partial state but do not satisfy R5.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-9.md:74:- How should R3 close? -> rec: Keep R3 pending; do not auto-delete directories.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-9.md:75:- Should NFR-S01 trigger live tool-permission narrowing? -> rec: Document now; do not auto-change live permissions in this remediation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-9.md:76:- When can 002 be marked complete? -> rec: Only after R3 evidence plus full R5 gates; package checks alone support partial status.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-9.md:78:RULES: Verify every claim against the real repo with file:line/command evidence. 'Reconcile-to-reality' is the default philosophy (docs should match what is actually on disk) unless you find a decisive reason to revert. Prefer reversible doc edits; flag live-infra edits and destructive ops (folder removal, tool-permission changes) explicitly. Be concrete: exact files, exact changes.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/feature_catalog/04--model-benchmark-mode/mode-switch.md:15:<!-- sk-doc-template: skill_asset_feature_catalog -->
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/feature_catalog/04--model-benchmark-mode/mode-switch.md:29:Mode resolution is closed-set. `VALID_MODES` holds only `agent-improvement` and `model-benchmark`, and `resolveMode()` treats an undefined mode as `agent-improvement`. An unknown mode value writes a warning to stderr and falls back to `agent-improvement`, so a typo degrades to the safe default rather than failing the run. The model-benchmark plan also requires `--profile` and `--outputs-dir`, and the host aborts remaining steps when an earlier step exits non-zero.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/feature_catalog/04--model-benchmark-mode/mode-switch.md:39:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs` | Mode router | Parses `--mode`, resolves the closed mode set, and plans the agent-improvement or model-benchmark step sequence. |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/feature_catalog/04--model-benchmark-mode/mode-switch.md:48:| `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/tests/loop-host.vitest.ts` | Automated test | Verifies `parseArgs`, `resolveMode`, the closed `VALID_MODES` set, the two-step model-benchmark plan, and the unknown-mode stderr fallback. |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-6.md:48:- S14: Replace 001 NFR-S01 with exact wording: `- **NFR-S01**: For an invokable parent-skill hub, allowed-tools is a family-level hub grant: it may be the union required by all routed mode packets. The mechanism must not move per-mode workflow logic, convergence/state behavior, or semantic tool-use boundaries into the hub, and any effective permission widening caused by Skill(<parent>) must be explicit in the decision record. A stricter per-mode runtime sandbox is a separate runtime enhancement, not part of Option E.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/001-invocability-mechanism/spec.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-6.md:49:- S15: Add this exact 001 decision-record wording after ADR-001 `Why this one`: `**Security / NFR-S01 evaluation**: Option E uses the parent hub's allowed-tools for parent invocation. That is an intentional parent-skill union grant, not proof that the runtime narrows to the selected packet's frontmatter. The hub stays routing-only; each mode packet retains its own behavior contract, but those contracts are not a separate runtime permission sandbox when reached through Skill(<parent>). For deep-loop, WebFetch must remain available to the family hub because the research mode declares and uses it. Therefore NFR-S01 is satisfied only as an explicit, documented union-grant contract. Runtime-enforced per-mode narrowing remains a future Option-D-style enhancement.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/001-invocability-mechanism/decision-record.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-6.md:50:- S16: Replace 002 NFR-S01 with exact wording: `- **NFR-S01**: Deep-loop alignment must preserve the parent-hub union-grant contract: the deep-loop-workflows hub may carry the union of tools needed by its modes, including WebFetch for deep-research. Alignment must not flatten per-mode workflow logic into the hub or change mode-owned instructions; inward-only modes remain inward-only by their packet contracts. The effective hub grant must be documented as broader than some mode frontmatters, and runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing is out of scope unless separately designed and verified.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-6.md:51:- S17: Add a new 002 decision-record section with exact wording: `## ADR-004: Parent-hub tool grant for NFR-S01\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Status** | Accepted - document union-grant contract |\n| **Date** | 2026-06-28 |\n\n### Context\n`deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md` is the invokable hub. Its allowed-tools includes WebFetch; `deep-research/SKILL.md` also includes WebFetch, while context/review/ai-council/improvement do not. The parent-skill hub template says hub allowed-tools is the union the modes need.\n\n### Decision\nKeep WebFetch on the deep-loop hub. Treat hub allowed-tools as the effective family-level grant for Skill(deep-loop-workflows); the hub remains routing-only and mode packets retain their own behavioral/tool-use contracts. Do not claim runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing unless a separate runtime enhancement proves it.\n\n### Consequences\n- This resolves NFR-S01 by documenting the effective contract instead of pretending the hub is an intersection grant.\n- Inward-only mode packets remain inward-only by instruction and review gates, not by a separate runtime permission sandbox when entered through the parent hub.\n- Removing WebFetch is rejected because it would break the deep-research route through the hub.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-6.md:52:- S18: Do not change live hub allowed-tools in this remediation pass; any runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing should be a separate high-blast design with tests. [.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md, .opencode/skills/sk-design/SKILL.md, .opencode/skills/sk-doc/assets/skill/parent_skill_hub_template.md] risk=med

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-6.md:74:RULES: Verify every claim against the real repo with file:line/command evidence. 'Reconcile-to-reality' is the default philosophy (docs should match what is actually on disk) unless you find a decisive reason to revert. .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:3:description: Policy for handling runtime mirror drift after canonical deep-improvement promotion.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:5:  - "mirror drift policy"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:7:  - "agent definition drift"
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:16:Policy reference for handling runtime mirror drift after canonical mutation. Use it when packaging parity must be reviewed without confusing mirror-sync work with evaluator truth.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:24:Defines how runtime-mirror drift should be reviewed and recorded after a canonical target changes.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:35:Mirror drift is real maintenance work, but it is downstream from experiment truth and must not be mistaken for benchmark evidence.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:43:For guarded promotion of agent definitions, downstream does not mean optional. Before an agent-definition candidate can be promoted, `.opencode/agents/`, `.claude/agents/`, and `.codex/agents/` must all contain the corresponding agent and must match the proposed body. Codex TOML is compared by its extracted `developer_instructions` body tokens so runtime wrapper metadata does not create false drift.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:51:1. Run a drift review against `.claude/agents/` and `.codex/agents/`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:60:Use `scan-integration.cjs` as the primary tool for detecting mirror drift. It replaces manual file-by-file comparison with automated signal-matching across all 3 runtime mirrors:
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/agent_improvement/mirror_drift_policy.md:74:- drift report plus follow-up packet

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:43:- When disabled it reports `status: unscored-mode-a` (never faked); when enabled it contributes its 12 points to the measured aggregate.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:52:**Live trace (Mode B)** replaces the D2/D3 router-replay proxies with the model's stated/observed routing (references only — assets are on the `assetRecall` lane). Signals that did not run report `status: unscored-mode-a` — never faked.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/references/skill_benchmark/scoring_contract.md:56:`score-skill-benchmark.cjs` emits `advisorySignals` in the machine report, and `build-report.cjs` renders them under **Advisory signals (NOT in the weighted aggregate)**. `D4_task_outcome` reports the opt-in D4-R routine-task usefulness delta when `--d4` live ablation runs; otherwise it is unscored. `assetRecall` reports expected deferred-asset support when live stated assets are available; otherwise it is deferred or unscored. Both are diagnostic only and never change `aggregateScore`, `dimensionScores`, `verdict`, or D4's weighted status.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/09--model-benchmark-mode/unknown-fallback.md:17:This scenario validates that an unknown `--scorer` value warns to stderr and defaults to `pattern`, and that an unknown `--mode` value warns to stderr and falls back to `agent-improvement`. Both fallbacks complete without crashing.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/09--model-benchmark-mode/unknown-fallback.md:27:- Expected signals: Unknown-scorer run completes with exit code 0; stderr contains `unknown --scorer 'bogus', defaulting to 'pattern'`; the unknown-scorer `report.json` has `scoringMethod: "pattern"`; unknown-mode run completes (does not crash); stderr contains `unknown mode 'bogus', defaulting to 'agent-improvement'`; the unknown-mode run routes to `score-candidate.cjs` and produces a score JSON, not a benchmark report

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:37:- S14: Replace 001 NFR-S01 with exact wording: `- **NFR-S01**: For an invokable parent-skill hub, allowed-tools is a family-level hub grant: it may be the union required by all routed mode packets. The mechanism must not move per-mode workflow logic, convergence/state behavior, or semantic tool-use boundaries into the hub, and any effective permission widening caused by Skill(<parent>) must be explicit in the decision record. A stricter per-mode runtime sandbox is a separate runtime enhancement, not part of Option E.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/001-invocability-mechanism/spec.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:38:- S15: Add this exact 001 decision-record wording after ADR-001 `Why this one`: `**Security / NFR-S01 evaluation**: Option E uses the parent hub's allowed-tools for parent invocation. That is an intentional parent-skill union grant, not proof that the runtime narrows to the selected packet's frontmatter. The hub stays routing-only; each mode packet retains its own behavior contract, but those contracts are not a separate runtime permission sandbox when reached through Skill(<parent>). For deep-loop, WebFetch must remain available to the family hub because the research mode declares and uses it. Therefore NFR-S01 is satisfied only as an explicit, documented union-grant contract. Runtime-enforced per-mode narrowing remains a future Option-D-style enhancement.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/001-invocability-mechanism/decision-record.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:39:- S16: Replace 002 NFR-S01 with exact wording: `- **NFR-S01**: Deep-loop alignment must preserve the parent-hub union-grant contract: the deep-loop-workflows hub may carry the union of tools needed by its modes, including WebFetch for deep-research. Alignment must not flatten per-mode workflow logic into the hub or change mode-owned instructions; inward-only modes remain inward-only by their packet contracts. The effective hub grant must be documented as broader than some mode frontmatters, and runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing is out of scope unless separately designed and verified.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:40:- S17: Add a new 002 decision-record section with exact wording: `## ADR-004: Parent-hub tool grant for NFR-S01\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Status** | Accepted - document union-grant contract |\n| **Date** | 2026-06-28 |\n\n### Context\n`deep-loop-workflows/SKILL.md` is the invokable hub. Its allowed-tools includes WebFetch; `deep-research/SKILL.md` also includes WebFetch, while context/review/ai-council/improvement do not. The parent-skill hub template says hub allowed-tools is the union the modes need.\n\n### Decision\nKeep WebFetch on the deep-loop hub. Treat hub allowed-tools as the effective family-level grant for Skill(deep-loop-workflows); the hub remains routing-only and mode packets retain their own behavioral/tool-use contracts. Do not claim runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing unless a separate runtime enhancement proves it.\n\n### Consequences\n- This resolves NFR-S01 by documenting the effective contract instead of pretending the hub is an intersection grant.\n- Inward-only mode packets remain inward-only by instruction and review gates, not by a separate runtime permission sandbox when entered through the parent hub.\n- Removing WebFetch is rejected because it would break the deep-research route through the hub.` [.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/002-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md] risk=low
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:41:- S18: Do not change live hub allowed-tools in this remediation pass; any runtime-enforced per-mode permission narrowing should be a separate high-blast design with tests. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:67:- Should the four non-council SKILL.md files gain explicit feature_catalog pointers? -> rec: Yes, but treat it as a small live-doc edit after the packet-doc reconciliation, because only deep-ai-council currently exposes the catalog from SKILL.md.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:72:- Should remediation run a live runtime probe for 001 now? -> rec: A. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:75:- How should R3 close? -> rec: Keep R3 pending until a per-mode table is added; if every mode earns its catalog, close R3 as keep-all with evidence rather than as an assumed keep-all.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:76:- When can 002 move from in_progress to complete? -> rec: Use the first option. Package checks support the 65% partial state but do not satisfy R5.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/155-parent-skill-native-invocability/research/prompts/iter-7.md:78:RULES: Verify every claim against the real repo with file:line/command evidence. 'Reconcile-to-reality' is the default philosophy (docs should match what is actually on disk) unless you find a decisive reason to revert. Prefer reversible doc edits; flag live-infra edits and destructive ops (folder removal, tool-permission changes) explicitly. Be concrete: exact files, exact changes.

.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/09--model-benchmark-mode/mode-switch-routing.md:27:- Expected signals: model-benchmark run completes with exit code 0; `materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs` emits `status: "fixtures-materialized"` before scoring; `run-benchmark.cjs` writes `report.json` with `status: "benchmark-complete"`; the benchmark `report.json` carries `mode` evidence via the appended `benchmark_run` row (`mode: "model-benchmark"`); the default-route run (no `--mode`) routes to `score-candidate.cjs` and never loads `dispatch-model.cjs`; unknown mode warns and falls back to agent-improvement
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/09--model-benchmark-mode/mode-switch-routing.md:29:- Pass/fail: The model-benchmark route produces a materialized fixture set plus a `benchmark-complete` report, and the default route stays on the agent-improvement scorer with no benchmark artifacts -- confirming the mode switch is wired and the default path is unchanged.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/09--model-benchmark-mode/mode-switch-routing.md:46:| MB-038 | Mode Switch Routing via loop-host | Validate Mode Switch Routing via loop-host | `Validate that loop-host routes model-benchmark mode to materialize plus run-benchmark and keeps agent-improvement default.` | rm -rf /tmp/mb-038 &amp;&amp; mkdir -p /tmp/mb-038/bench &amp;&amp; \<br>node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs \<br>  --mode=model-benchmark \<br>  --profile=.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/assets/model_benchmark/benchmark-profiles/default.json \<br>  --outputs-dir=/tmp/mb-038/bench \<br>  --output=/tmp/mb-038/bench/report.json \<br>  --state-log=/tmp/mb-038/improvement/agent-improvement-state.jsonl ; \<br>node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs \<br>  --candidate=.opencode/agents/debug.md \<br>  --output=/tmp/mb-038/agent-score.json | model-benchmark run completes with exit code 0; `materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs` emits `status: "fixtures-materialized"`; `report.json` has `status: "benchmark-complete"`; appended `benchmark_run` row carries `mode: "model-benchmark"`; default-route run routes to `score-candidate.cjs` and produces a score JSON without benchmark fields; unknown mode warns and falls back to agent-improvement | `terminal transcript, command output, generated files, and PASS/FAIL verdict` | The model-benchmark route materializes fixtures and writes a `benchmark-complete` report while the default route stays on the agent-improvement scorer -- confirming the mode switch routes correctly and the default path is unchanged. | If `report.json` is missing or all fixtures score 0 with `missing-output`: confirm `materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs` ran first and wrote the `.md` files into `--outputs-dir`<br>If the default route produces benchmark fields: check `planInvocation()` in `loop-host.cjs` for mode resolution<br>If unknown mode does not warn: verify `resolveMode()` writes the fallback notice to stderr |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/03--5d-scorer/missing-candidate.md:17:This scenario validates that providing a nonexistent candidate file results in an infra_failure status and exit code 1.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/03--5d-scorer/missing-candidate.md:24:- Real user request: `Validate that providing a nonexistent candidate file results in an infra_failure status and exit code 1.`
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/03--5d-scorer/missing-candidate.md:27:- Expected signals: Exit code is 1 (not 0); Output is valid JSON (no stack trace); `status` field equals `"infra_failure"`; `failureModes` array contains `"profile-generation-failure"`; No unhandled exception or stack trace is printed
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/03--5d-scorer/missing-candidate.md:29:- Pass/fail: Graceful error output (valid JSON) with `status: "infra_failure"`, `failureModes: ["profile-generation-failure"]`, and exit code 1. No stack trace in output.
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/manual_testing_playbook/03--5d-scorer/missing-candidate.md:46:| 5D-011 | Missing Candidate File Returns infra_failure | Validate Missing Candidate File Returns infra_failure | `Validate that a missing candidate file returns infra_failure and exit code 1.` | node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/agent-improvement/score-candidate.cjs \<br>  --candidate=nonexistent-file.md --dynamic; echo &quot;Exit: $?&quot; | Exit code is 1 (not 0); Output is valid JSON (no stack trace); `status` field equals `"infra_failure"`; `failureModes` array contains `"profile-generation-failure"`; No unhandled exception or stack trace is printed | `terminal transcript, command output, generated files, and PASS/FAIL verdict` | Graceful error output (valid JSON) with `status: "infra_failure"`, `failureModes: ["profile-generation-failure"]`, and exit code 1. No stack trace in output. | If exit code is 0: the script is not validating file existence before scoring<br>If output is not valid JSON or a stack trace appears: add try/catch around the file-read step with proper error classification and JSON error envelope<br>If exit code is 2 instead of 1: review the exit code convention (1 = infra failure, 2 = scoring failure)<br>If `failureModes` is missing or empty: check the error path in the dynamic profile generation pipeline |
